name: Streamline a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive 24/7

parameters.message: =✅ Streamline a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive  — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 12 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Payload Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Fetch Changes Since Watermark · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fetch Changes Since Watermark

GET https://api.example.com/v1/payloads — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Normalize Records · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Normalize Records

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming payload records to a stable schema

Next: Upsert into Google Sheets

### Upsert into Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Payload Tracker', matched on `itemId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Advance Watermark · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Advance Watermark

Custom JavaScript: Advance the incremental watermark to the newest record seen

Next: Persist Watermark (Google Sheets)

### Persist Watermark (Google Sheets)

Upserts a row into 'Payload Tracker', matched on `itemId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Guard Non-Empty source · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Guard Non-Empty source

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Payload Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Twilio · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Streamline a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive 24/7

**Pattern:** Workflow Automation · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Streamline a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive 24/7. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual helpers is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Load Previous Snapshot → Fetch Changes Since Watermark → Normalize Records → Upsert into Google Sheets → Advance Watermark → Persist Watermark (Google Sheets) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to workflow automation without manual effort
- Anyone automating helpers in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The pipeline is incremental, not full-refresh: it stores a watermark (the last-seen timestamp) and only asks the source for records newer than that, which is what keeps a scheduled re-run cheap instead of re-pulling the entire dataset every time. The tradeoff is that a failed watermark write must be safe to retry — which is exactly why the destination write is an upsert, not a plain insert.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Manually roll the watermark back and re-run — confirm the upsert makes the replay safe (no duplicates).
2. Trigger a reconcile mismatch on purpose and confirm the drift alarm fires.
3. Point the source at a different platform and see how little else in the workflow has to change.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your helpers process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
